description: §14.8.9 egress redaction — a server fn whose SELECT is prefixed by a leading SQL block comment (`/* fetch user */ SELECT * FROM users ...`) over a protect= table still resolves its origin (the comment is stripped before the leader test) and strips the protected column, firing I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 naming `passwordHash`. REGRESSION GUARD for the S249 fail-open: the leader test previously required the query text to START with `SELECT`, so a comment-prefixed row returned no protected-column tag and `passwordHash` SHIPPED unredacted. The comment-strip half of the fix (the CTE half is cte-strip-info).
rationale: §14.8.9 keys redaction on a column's resolved (table,column) origin. A leading SQL comment is not part of the query's semantics — after it is stripped the query is a plain single-table SELECT *, whose protected-origin column `passwordHash` is tagged and stripped at egress; I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 names the column. A leading comment must never let a protected-origin row slip past the egress gate untagged.
